summary: Excellent audience recording of an underrated 1982 show featuring a stellar Bertha/Roses opener and a blistering Stranger > Franklin’s Tower > Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World sequence.
review: The tape captures a solid 1982 concert with crisp, well‑balanced audience sound that upgrades previous releases. Fans rave about Jerry’s soulful vocals on “It Must Have Been the Roses,” creative guitar work in the second‑set medley, and inventive segues from Space into Truckin' and Black Peter.

ai_rating.rationale: A detailed 5‑star review praises both the musical performance (standout songs, inventive playing) and the recording quality (great audience capture), indicating an exceptional show with excellent audio.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Audience recording described as a ‘definite archive upgrade’; clear, well‑balanced and no significant flaws mentioned.

standout_songs: It Must Have Been the Roses
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds with a strong Bertha and heartfelt Roses; second set erupts in a fiery Stranger > Franklin’s Tower > Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World run, followed by creative segues through Space into Truckin' and Black Peter.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for poignant vocals and soulful solo on Roses; guitar work highlighted throughout especially in Franklin's Tower and Eyes of the World.
